generated_oml:
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the motor shaft for measuring motor angle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorAngleDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox for measuring output angle"
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxOutputAngleDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring motor current (IM) as electrical control input"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorAngleDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time motor angle data transmitted via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance GearboxOutputAngleDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Output angle data from gearbox encoder transmitted via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoder
]

instance CurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor current (IM) data transmitted via MQTT protocol"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance MotorAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor shaft angle measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorAngleDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool, CPlusPlusLanguage, JavaLanguage, SysMLStateMachines, MontiArcAutomaton
]

instance GearboxOutputAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Output angle measurements after gearbox"
    DTDFVocab:fromData GearboxOutputAngleDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool, CPlusPlusLanguage, JavaLanguage, SysMLStateMachines, MontiArcAutomaton
]

instance CurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor current (IM) measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkTool, CPlusPlusLanguage, JavaLanguage, SysMLStateMachines, MontiArcAutomaton
]

]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first axis of the MABI MAX 100 robot"
]

instance PrimaryGear : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary gear in the gear transmission system"
]

instance CycloidalGear : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gear in the gear transmission system"
]

instance GearTransmissionSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Gear transmission system comprising primary and cycloidal gears"
    base:contains PrimaryGear, CycloidalGear
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK840DslController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller for precise motion control"
]

instance MABIMAX100Robot : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "MABI MAX 100 serial robot with six axes for complex manufacturing tasks"
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearTransmissionSystem, SiemensSINUMERIK840DslController
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Architecture description languages (ADLs) such as ACME, AADL, MontiArc, and SysML IBDs model component & connector perspectives for functional and logical architecture, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral aspects are described using MontiArc Automaton or SysML state machines. Methodologies like RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, and SMArDT handle complexity through abstraction levels (requirements, functional, logical, product/technical layers), requiring integration of heterogeneous tools and stakeholders for comprehensive MBSE descriptions."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol is used for streaming sensor time series data between physical and digital components."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system complies with industry standards including SysML [17], AADL [24], ACME [23], MontiArc [25, 26], and methodologies such as RFLP [18, 19], OOSEM [20], SPES [21], and SMArDT [22]. These standards support interoperability and integration of heterogeneous models and tools (e.g., Simulink, C++, Java) while addressing diverse stakeholder perspectives [31]."
]
